name: Supply Network Twin & Scenario Command

scenarios.title: Create a durable supply network twin packet
steps: Open the Sprint 7 Supply Network Twin workspace.
Review twin graph nodes/edges, risks, scenario drafts, network design, simulation, planning, resilience, order, shipment, inventory, supplier, customer, and action queue signals.
expected: A durable AssistantSupplyNetworkTwinPacket row is created with source summary, graph coverage, network baseline, scenario command, bottlenecks, disruptions, recovery playbooks, confidence, response plan, rollback plan, and leadership summary JSON.
An AssistantAuditEvent records that packet creation is evidence-only.
No order, inventory, WMS task, shipment, supplier, customer, warehouse, route, RFQ, tariff, twin graph row, scenario draft, network plan, or customer promise is changed automatically.

scenarios.title: Queue supply network scenario review
steps: Add an optional network scenario review note to a draft Sprint 7 packet.
expected: The packet moves to REVIEW_QUEUED and stores actionQueueItemId.
An AssistantActionQueueItem is created for supply_network_twin_review with the packet URL and source evidence.

Review work remains separate from execution; the assistant does not promote scenarios or mutate orders, inventory, shipments, suppliers, warehouses, routes, network plans, twin graph records, or customer promises.

scenarios.title: Approve a reviewed Sprint 7 packet
steps: Review the packet leadership summary, graph confidence, bottlenecks, disruptions, recovery playbooks, and rollback plan.
expected: The packet status becomes APPROVED with approvedAt, approvedByUserId, and approvalNote.
An AssistantAuditEvent records approval and reiterates the no-silent-mutation guardrail.
Approval marks the network scenario evidence packet as reviewed only; execution still requires separate downstream approved actions.

guardrails: No twin graph node, edge, risk signal, or scenario draft is created or changed automatically.
No order, sales order, purchase order, shipment, WMS task, inventory, warehouse, route, RFQ, tariff, supplier, or customer record is changed automatically.
No scenario is promoted into planning, procurement, transport, warehouse, finance, or customer-facing execution automatically.
No customer promise or external stakeholder communication is changed or sent automatically.
Recovery playbooks create review work only and require separate downstream approval before execution.
